title: Thread safety model is well-documented and consistent
description:
The lora_l2.c threading model demonstrates careful design:

**Clear ownership rules (lines 84-103):**
- Atomic fields for fast-path checks without mutex
- Mutex-protected fields with explicit documentation
- Callback + user_data always updated as a pair

**Lock ordering documented (lichen_l2.c:436-439):**
- tx_mutex before rx_mutex, consistently
- Comment references issue tracker for audit trail

**Callback snapshot pattern (lora_l2.c:258-265):**
```c
k_mutex_lock(&lora_mutex, K_FOREVER);
lichen_lora_rx_cb_t cb = lora_state.rx_callback;
void *cb_user_data = lora_state.rx_callback_user_data;
k_mutex_unlock(&lora_mutex);

if (cb) {
    cb(rx_buf, ret, rssi, snr, cb_user_data);
}
```
Snapshots callback pointer pair under lock, then invokes without lock. Prevents deadlock if callback tries to modify registration.

**Stop-then-join with cleanup ordering (lora_l2.c:399-444):**
- Clears callback before signaling exit
- Releases mutex before join to allow in-flight TX to complete
- Documents that any in-flight callback will still run (and that is OK)

This is production-quality concurrency design worth preserving.
close_reason: Positive finding - no action needed, documents good practice
